title: quotewildcard: backslash not escaped per RFC 5229 §4.1.2
description: TRIGGER: set :quotewildcard applied to a value containing a backslash character, e.g. value = 'path\to\file'. BAD BEHAVIOR: RFC 5229 §4.1.2 states 'Every character with special meaning (*,  ?, and \) is prefixed with \ in the expansion.' Current code only replaces '*' and '?' but does NOT escape backslash. A backslash in the value is left unescaped, meaning that if the result is used in a :matches test, the backslash will be interpreted as a glob escape character rather than a literal backslash. This is a MUST-level requirement in the RFC (the word 'every' is unambiguous).
